response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
I'm not sure how much I should say on this, but I know that people are talking a lot about the vaccines because of everything going on. It seems like there's a mix of emotions and opinions swirling around everywhere you look.

When it comes to these kinds of conversations, maybe just listen without pushing my own views too hard. That way, everyone feels heard. After all, I'm still figuring things out myself due to feeling depressed sometimes; not having much education doesn't help either in understanding complex topics like this one. My parents have different levels - Dad didn't go to school at all while Mom went as far as a bachelor's degree. Maybe that affects how they approach these discussions.

I'd suggest being open-minded, which I score pretty high on openness according to my test results. So perhaps try learning from others even if it makes me uncomfortable sometimes because agreeableness is strong here too. But with low conscientiousness and moderate depression weighing in, staying calm during debates isn't always easy.

As for religious beliefs being Islam - well, I'm not sure how much that comes up in these chats unless someone brings religion into the mix intentionally.

Since I don't spend time on social media myself due to my depressed state or lack of interest maybe all those online arguments aren't really affecting me directly. But knowing about hobbies like birdwatching and hiking might help find ways to cope when discussions get too much.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage COVID vaccine discussions, one should adopt an open-minded approach considering personal traits such as high openness score (72) from the Big Five personality test; moderate agreeableness (91) suggests being cooperative during conversations. Personal background like depressed mood and upper secondary education may influence understanding capacity while Islam religious beliefs might be relevant if tied to cultural norms around healthcare.
response_parsed.tone: Calm, empathetic yet cautious due to personal mental health challenges
